title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Qualifications

Quantitative background in Mathematical Engineering with strong foundations in statistics, optimization, and applied analytics
Experience in data analysis and modeling through academic projects and a research internship — including decision trees, network analysis, and financial modeling
Programming proficiency in Python, R, SQL, and Excel
Proactive, disciplined, and motivated to contribute in data-driven professional environments

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Education

Boston University — Advanced Graduate Certificate in Applied Business Analytics
Boston, MA, USA | Expected May 2026
Coursework: Data Mining, Marketing Analytics, Enterprise Risk Analytics, Data Visualization, SQL & R for Business Analytics

Madrid, Spain | Expected July 2026
Coursework: Probability and Statistics, Inferential Statistics, Data Analysis, Optimization, Stochastic Calculus, Financial Mathematics

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Relevant Projects

Loan Amortization Models Project
CEU San Pablo University

Built an interactive tool to compare French, German, and American loan amortization models
Applied financial mathematics and data analysis to generate customized repayment scenarios
Supported data-driven financial decision-making



PageRank Algorithm Project
CEU San Pablo University

Implemented the PageRank algorithm to analyze network structures and rank node importance
Applied linear algebra and graph theory to solve real-world data ranking problems
Demonstrated practical applications of algorithms in data-driven decision contexts

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:

Research Intern — Spanish National Research Council (CSIC)
Madrid, Spain | July 2025

Designed and implemented a decision tree model to guide the selection of appropriate statistical tests
Applied probability, inferential statistics, and data analysis to improve analytical workflows
Developed practical analytical tools that contributed to data-driven decision-making within a research environment
